documents.title: Academic Use of Artificial Intelligence at BYU

ai_strategy.summary: BYU maintains a dedicated Generative Artificial Intelligence site that centralizes safe-use guidance, training, academic integrity expectations, copyright considerations, and service information. Its academic guidance permits instructor-level decisions while emphasizing responsible use, privacy, copyright, and student accountability.

key_provisions: Use AI in accordance with academic integrity expectations and instructor or unit guidance
Do not enter restricted, confidential, regulated, or sensitive data into unapproved AI tools
Review AI outputs for accuracy, hallucination, bias, copyright, and attribution issues
Disclose or cite AI use when required by course, research, publisher, or unit rules
Keep human judgment and accountability in consequential academic, research, and administrative decisions
ai_policy.summary: Brigham Young University's AI policy posture is best characterized as permissive: public sources show explicit expectations for responsible use, privacy, disclosure, academic integrity, and human review, while leaving some implementation decisions to units or instructors.

bodies.name: Generative AI Committee / university task force
bodies.scope: Campus guidance and policy recommendations for safe and responsible GenAI use.

bodies.name: BYU GenAI hub
bodies.scope: Central public resource for training, safe use, academic integrity, copyright, and GenAI services.

governance_structure.summary: Brigham Young University's public AI governance is organized through Generative AI Committee / university task force, BYU GenAI hub.

computing_infrastructure.supercomputer_name: BYU Office of Research Computing clusters
computing_infrastructure.summary: BYU Research Computing publishes cluster resource tables with 367 total GPUs, including H200, H100, B200, A100, L40S, V100, P100, and T4 resources. The page is a general research-computing inventory rather than a separately branded AI supercomputer.

student_facing_ai.name: BYU GenAI student guidance
student_facing_ai.scope: Students and faculty using GenAI in coursework and research.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Safe and responsible GenAI training
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: The GenAI hub frames safe use, copyright, academic integrity, and training as broad campus resources rather than discipline-only support.

regulatory_context.summary: Brigham Young University operates in Utah; public AI guidance should be read alongside applicable state, federal, and institutional privacy, security, accessibility, and academic-integrity requirements.

narrative_summary: Brigham Young University (Provo, UT) is an R1 institution whose public AI posture shows activity across governance, teaching, research, enterprise tooling, workforce readiness, and access. BYU maintains a dedicated Generative Artificial Intelligence site that centralizes safe-use guidance, training, academic integrity expectations, copyright considerations, and service information. Its academic guidance permits instructor-level decisions while emphasizing responsible use, privacy, copyright, and student accountability. The most concrete public initiatives captured here are BYU GenAI hub; Academic Use of Artificial Intelligence guidance; AI and Machine Learning research area; Artificial Intelligence in Medicine course; Humanities teaching with AI resources. Across the profile, the strongest evidence comes from official university AI hubs, policy pages, teaching resources, research-center pages, and program pages rather than secondary reporting. The institution_type is set to r1_research from the canonical Carnegie R1 list. Known gaps are intentionally explicit: leadership names, user counts, detailed budgets, and compute counts are only included when they surfaced in public sources. Overall, the profile supports at least five AI initiatives and evidences strategy/leadership, governance, teaching/curriculum, research infrastructure, student services or enterprise tooling, workforce/community, partnerships, and equity dimensions where public sources permit.

gaps_acknowledged: No public BYU chief AI officer was found.

BYU's research-computing GPU inventory and Microsoft Copilot Enterprise access are public, but no BYU-wide disclosed AI investment amount, Copilot contract value, exact active user count, or assessment-redesign outcome metric surfaced.
Enrollment was left null rather than relying on non-source local data.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: governance led.
